rationale: det_new_equal requires r1.un_acked == r2.un_acked where un_acked: Vec<CSingleMessage>. CAckState::new() only ensures un_acked.len()==0 and r@ =~= AckState::new() (which lifts to Seq view equality of un_acked). However, Vec<T> in vstd is #[verifier::external_body] and has no axiom relating raw == equality to view equality. I verified this with minimal test programs: two Vec values with identical (empty) views are not provably ==. The standard lemmas (lemma_vec_obeys_view_eq, group_vec_axioms) only relate the PartialEq eq_spec method to view equality, not raw struct == equality. Therefore the determinism check generated for CAckState::new() is undischargeable in Verus without assume(), admit(), or an external_body lemma, all of which are forbidden by the task constraints. The proof block in det.rs makes the strongest progress possible (Seq view equality and CAckState ext_equal field-by-field), but the final r1.un_acked == r2.un_acked step remains an open assertion that the SMT cannot clo
